oai_dc:dc.dc:title: In Memoriam ~ Luana Feigelstock
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Robinson, Sheryl
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: This item originally appeared in AFA Watchbird Volume 51 Number 1 (January/February 2024).The following is an excerpt from the full PDF link.Luana Feigelstockby Sheryl Robinson Luana Feigelstock (12/27/1942 - 12/17/2023) was a dedicated woman with a passion for the birds of the Brotogeris species, specifically the Grey-cheeked Parakeet (Brotogeris pyrrhopterus).Like many others, Luana saw her first Grey -cheeked Parakeet in a pet store. Smitten with their engaging personality, color, and size, she could not choose one, and left empty -handed. Months later, she purchased a Grey -cheek, who she named NER. As the years passed, her interest in the Grey-cheeked Parakeet grew... Sadly, NER passed away in 1999. Determined never to be without another one again, Luana learned to breed the Grey -cheeked Parakeet herself. Searching the internet, she found a group of pet owners, and breeders with the same passion for theClick the PDF link associated with this item to view the complete item as originally published.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: AFA Watchbird; Vol. 51 No. 1 (2024): Watchbird January/February 2024; 20
